# WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0002 - QUIC RFC 9001 TLS KeyUpdate Classifier Work Item

## Summary

Add the narrow post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate classifier seam, preserve the separate managed key-phase install path, and route prohibited KeyUpdate messages into the existing KeyUpdateError close behavior.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0002

## Planned Changes

- Add a TLS KeyUpdate post-handshake classifier in QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.
- Publish a prohibited KeyUpdate update from the bridge driver.
- Keep QuicTransportTlsBridgeState and QuicConnectionRuntime on the existing close path.
- Add positive and negative requirement-home coverage for the real message-processing seam.
- Update the RFC 9001 gap ledger and spec trace to point the 0009/0010 proof at this slice.

## Out of Scope

- Broad TLS post-handshake feature support.
- Changing the managed QUIC key-phase install path.
- Adding public API or interop surface for TLS KeyUpdate.

## Verification Plan

Run the RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests for the prohibited TLS KeyUpdate and connection-error clauses, confirm the real TLS message-processing seam emits the prohibited-update state for a TLS KeyUpdate message, confirm the NewSessionTicket path still publishes ticket state, and render plus validate the RFC 9001 SpecTrace JSON artifacts.

## Completion Notes

Completed for the narrow TLS KeyUpdate classifier slice. The requirement-home tests prove the prohibited KeyUpdate path while NewSessionTicket remains separately supported and the managed key-phase install path stays distinct.
